name: Philodendron atabapoense
aboutText: Philodendron atabapoense is a striking hemiepiphyte native to the shaded riverine forests of the upper Orinoco region in Venezuela and Colombia. It is characterized by its dramatic, elongated leaves that feature a deep, glossy green upper surface contrasted by a stunning deep maroon underside. As a climber, it produces slender internodes and active aerial roots, making it an excellent candidate for growth on moss poles or totem supports. This species is highly valued by collectors for its elegant, architectural silhouette and distinct coloration.

fieldNotes.title: Orinoco Tributary Origins
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Named for the Atabapo River in southern Venezuela — a tributary of the Orinoco that runs through one of the more botanically productive but under-surveyed regions of the continent. Philodendron atabapoense has bicoloured leaves: deep green above, distinctively red or burgundy on the underside — a contrast that is most apparent when the light catches the underside of a mature leaf. At accessible price tier for a plant with genuine visual distinction and an interesting provenance. Worth growing on a proper climbing structure to develop the mature leaf form.
